questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 58° and angle B measures 67°. What is the measure of angle C?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. Therefore, angle C = 180° − 58° − 67° = 55°.

questions.question: A right triangle has one acute angle measuring 34°. What is the measure of the other acute angle?
questions.explanation: In a right triangle, the two acute angles are complementary (sum to 90°). The other acute angle = 90° − 34° = 56°.

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has a vertex angle of 42°. What is the measure of each base angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the two base angles are equal. If the vertex angle is 42°, then each base angle = (180° − 42°) ÷ 2 = 69°.

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One of the alternate interior angles measures 123°. What is the measure of the other alternate interior angle?
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles formed by parallel lines and a transversal are congruent. Both angles measure 123°.

questions.question: In triangle PQR, side PQ = 8, side QR = 10, and side PR = 12. Which angle in the triangle is the largest?
questions.explanation: In any triangle, the largest angle is opposite the longest side. Since PR = 12 is the longest side, the angle opposite it (angle Q) is the largest.

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle measures 128°. One of the non-adjacent interior angles measures 47°. What is the measure of the other non-adjacent interior angle?
questions.explanation: By the exterior angle theorem, an exterior angle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles. So 128° = 47° + other angle; other angle = 81°.
